parser.tasks_appear_when_local_date_falls_insid: Tasks appear when the local date falls inside their planning-cycle window. Overdue unfinished tasks and habit actions also roll forward into non-future schedule days. Events appear when their scheduled time overlaps that day.
parser.the_output_groups_tasks_habit_actions_an: The output groups tasks, habit actions, and event occurrences for the day.
parser.use_list_when_you_need_same_schedule_vie: Use `list` when you need the same schedule view across an inclusive date range.
parser.use_either_repeated_date_or_start_date_e: Use either repeated `--date` or `--start-date/--end-date`, not both.
parser.use_show_for_one_exact_local_day: Use `show` for one exact local day.
parser.use_show_when_you_want_single_day_entryp: Use `show` when you want the single-day entrypoint with the same sections.
parser.when_date_is_omitted_show_uses_current_c: When `--date` is omitted, `show` uses the current configured local date.

parser.a_tag_is_scoped_by_name_entity_type_and_: A tag is scoped by name, entity type, and category.
parser.create_and_maintain_tags_for_notes_peopl: Create and maintain tags for notes, people, visions, tasks, and areas.
parser.delete_multiple_tags_in_one_command: Delete multiple tags in one command.
parser.see_lifeos_tag_batch_help_for_bulk_delet: See `lifeos tag batch --help` for bulk delete operations.
parser.show_one_tag_with_full_metadata: Show one tag with full metadata.
parser.tags_are_intended_for_structured_labelin: Tags are intended for structured labeling, not free-form note content.
parser.tags_provide_cross_cutting_classificatio: Tags provide cross-cutting classification across otherwise separate domains.
parser.use_clear_description_clear_color_or_cle: Use `--clear-description`, `--clear-color`, or `--clear-people` to remove optional values.
parser.use_entity_type_and_category_to_narrow_r: Use entity type and category to narrow the result set before inspecting one tag in detail.
parser.use_include_deleted_to_review_previously: Use `--include-deleted` to review previously deleted tags.

parser.create_and_maintain_task_trees_that_belo: Create and maintain task trees that belong to a vision.
parser.see_lifeos_task_batch_help_for_bulk_dele: See `lifeos task batch --help` for bulk delete operations.
parser.tasks_are_main_execution_unit_in_lifeos_: Tasks are the main execution unit in LifeOS and can be nested under parent tasks.
parser.use_lifeos_event_add_task_id_task_id_whe: Use `lifeos event add --task-id <task-id>` when the task also needs a specific appointment, timeblock, or deadline.
parser.use_planning_cycle_fields_for_broader_ti: Use planning-cycle fields for the broader timebox. Use `event` when the task also needs a concrete time block.

parser_batch.delete_multiple_tasks_in_one_command: Delete multiple tasks in one command.

parser_read_models.filter_by_planning_cycle_start_date_yyyy: Filter by planning cycle start date YYYY-MM-DD
parser_read_models.list_tasks_with_optional_vision_parent_o: List tasks with optional vision, parent, or status filters.
parser_read_models.show_all_active_tasks_for_vision_as_hier: Show all active tasks for a vision as a hierarchy.
parser_read_models.show_one_task_with_full_metadata: Show one task with full metadata.
parser_read_models.show_one_task_with_its_active_nested_sub: Show one task with its active nested subtasks.
parser_read_models.show_subtree_completion_and_effort_stati: Show subtree completion and effort statistics for one task.
parser_read_models.the_output_starts_with_vision_identifier: The output starts with the vision identifier, then prints each root task and descendant using the same indented task tree shape as `with-subtasks`.
parser_read_models.the_root_task_prints_first_followed_by_a: The root task prints first, followed by active descendants indented by depth with columns: task_id, status, completion_percentage, content.
parser_read_models.totals_aggregate_selected_task_together_: Totals aggregate the selected task together with all active descendants.
parser_read_models.use_hierarchy_when_you_need_full_active_: Use `hierarchy` when you need the full active tree for an entire vision.
parser_read_models.use_this_command_as_primary_query_entryp: Use this command as the primary query entrypoint for structured task views.
parser_read_models.use_with_subtasks_when_you_want_to_inspe: Use `with-subtasks` when you want to inspect only one branch of the tree.
parser_read_models.when_task_has_direct_children_completion: When the task has direct children, `completion_percentage` measures how many of those children are done.
parser_read_models.when_vision_id_is_provided_without_paren: When `--vision-id` is provided without `--parent-task-id`, only root tasks are listed.

parser_structure.move_task_to_new_parent_and_optionally_n: Move a task to a new parent and optionally a new vision.
parser_structure.move_task_to_root_level: Move the task to the root level
parser_structure.task_order_in_task_id_display_order_form: Task order in <task-id>:<display-order> format; repeat for multiple tasks

parser_structure.use_move_when_parentage_or_vision_member: Use `move` when parentage or vision membership also needs to change.
parser_structure.use_old_parent_task_id_as_optimistic_gua: Use `--old-parent-task-id` as an optimistic guard when another writer may have already moved the task.
parser_structure.use_reorder_when_only_sibling_display_or: Use `reorder` when only sibling display order changes and parentage stays the same.

parser_write.parent_task_references_must_stay_within_: Parent task references must stay within the same vision.
parser_write.planning_cycle_duration_in_days_for_encl: Planning cycle duration in days for the enclosing timebox
parser_write.planning_cycle_fields_describe_enclosing: Planning-cycle fields describe the enclosing timebox for the task, not a clock-time execution slot.
parser_write.planning_cycle_flags_must_be_supplied_as: Planning-cycle flags must be supplied as a complete set when used.
parser_write.planning_cycle_type_year_month_week_or_d: Planning cycle type: year, month, week, or day
parser_write.start_date_of_enclosing_planning_cycle_w: Start date of the enclosing planning cycle window in YYYY-MM-DD format
parser_write.tasks_can_be_root_tasks_or_child_tasks_u: Tasks can be root tasks or child tasks under another task in the same vision.
parser_write.updated_planning_cycle_duration_in_days_: Updated planning cycle duration in days for the enclosing timebox
parser_write.updated_planning_cycle_fields_still_desc: Updated planning-cycle fields still describe the enclosing timebox, not a specific scheduled timestamp.
parser_write.updated_planning_cycle_type_year_month_w: Updated planning cycle type: year, month, week, or day
parser_write.updated_start_date_of_enclosing_planning: Updated start date of the enclosing planning cycle window in YYYY-MM-DD format
parser_write.use_clear_flags_to_remove_optional_value: Use `--clear-*` flags to remove optional values such as descriptions or planning cycles.
parser_write.use_clear_parent_to_move_child_task_back: Use `--clear-parent` to move a child task back to the root level.
parser_write.use_lifeos_event_add_task_id_task_id_if_: Use `lifeos event add --task-id <task-id>` if the task also needs a specific time block on a calendar day.
parser_write.use_planning_cycle_fields_for_broader_ye: Use planning-cycle fields for the broader year, month, week, or day window that the task belongs to.
parser_write.use_repeated_person_id_to_keep_human_onl: Use repeated `--person-id` to keep human-only, agent-only, and shared task ownership explicit.
parser_write.when_agent_creates_tasks_on_behalf_of_hu: When an agent creates tasks on behalf of a human, use `--person-id` to mark whether the task belongs to the human, the agent, or both.

config_commands.agents_should_inspect_preference_languag: Agents should inspect `Preference language` before writing human-authored titles, descriptions, or note content.
config_commands.agents_should_use_effective_language_for: Agents should use the effective language for human-authored payload data unless the human explicitly overrides it.
config_commands.database_urls_hide_passwords_by_default_: Database URLs hide passwords by default. Use --show-secrets when needed.
config_commands.environment_variables_still_override_con: Environment variables still override config-file values at runtime.

init_commands.default_vision_experience_points_gained_: Default vision experience points gained per hour of actual effort
